 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{173.6}{{562.8} - {3.34} - {105.86}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{173.6}{453.6}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.38}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {7.44}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.38}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {353}  \quad n_{B_1} = {15}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {343}  \quad n_{B_2} = {15}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.82}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{353}^2}{15} + \frac{{343}^2}{15}} - \frac{{696}^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{124609}}{15} + \frac{{117649}}{15}} - \frac{{484416}}{30}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{8307.27} + {7843.27}} - {16147.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {16150.54} - {16147.2}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {3.34}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{3.34}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {3.34}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.34}{{562.8} - {173.6} - {105.86}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3.34}{283.34}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {0.29}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.61}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {282.8} - {173.6} - {3.34}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {105.86}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{105.86}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {52.93}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{105.86}{{562.8} - {173.6} - {3.34}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{105.86}{385.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.27}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {24}) = {4.54}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.27}\)

Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1083}  \quad n_{A_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1225}  \quad n_{A_2} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1083}^2}{28} + \frac{{1225}^2}{28}} - \frac{{2308}^2}{56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1172889}}{28} + \frac{{1500625}}{28}} - \frac{{5326864}}{56}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{41888.89} + {53593.75}} - {95122.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {95482.64} - {95122.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {360.07}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{360.07}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {360.07}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{360.07}{53.83}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {6.69}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1738.29}{53.83}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {32.29}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1738.29}{{5413.43} - {360.07} - {516.08}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1738.29}{4537.28}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.38}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {52}) = {32.29}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.38}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{516.08}{{5413.43} - {360.07} - {1738.29}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{516.08}{3315.07}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.16}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {52}) = {9.59}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 11
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {109} \quad M_{A1B1} = {15.57}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {13.71} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {122} \quad M_{A1B2} = {17.43}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {31.71} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {74} \quad M_{A2B1} = {10.57}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {21.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {135} \quad M_{A2B2} = {19.29}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {17.43} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {28} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {27}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {28} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {24}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7294} - \frac{440^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7294} - \frac{193600}{28}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7294} - {6914.29}\)  \(SS_{total} = {379.71}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {13.71 + 31.71 + 21.71 + 17.43}\)  \(SS_{within} = {84.56}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {379.71} - {84.56}\)  \(SS_{between} = {295.15}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {231}  \quad n_{A_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {209}  \quad n_{A_2} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.82}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{231}^2}{14} + \frac{{209}^2}{14}} - \frac{{440}^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{53361}}{14} + \frac{{43681}}{14}} - \frac{{193600}}{28}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{3811.5} + {3120.07}} - {6914.29}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {6931.57} - {6914.29}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {17.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{17.28}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {17.28}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{17.28}{3.52}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {4.91}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.28}{{379.71} - {195.57} - {82.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{17.28}{101.84}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {4.91}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {183}  \quad n_{B_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {257}  \quad n_{B_2} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.82}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{183}^2}{14} + \frac{{257}^2}{14}} - \frac{{440}^2}{28}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{33489}}{14} + \frac{{66049}}{14}} - \frac{{193600}}{28}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{2392.07} + {4717.79}} - {6914.29}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {7109.86} - {6914.29}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {195.57}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{195.57}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {195.57}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{195.57}{3.52}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {55.56}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{195.57}{{379.71} - {17.28} - {82.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{195.57}{280.13}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.7}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {55.56}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.7}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.82}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {295.15} - {17.28} - {195.57}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {82.3}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{82.3}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {82.3}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{82.3}{3.52}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {23.38}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{82.3}{{379.71} - {17.28} - {195.57}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{82.3}{166.86}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.49}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {24}) = {23.38}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.49}\)

text:

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {222485} - \frac{5045^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {222485} - \frac{25452025}{117}\)  \(SS_{total} = {222485} - {217538.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4946.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {427.69 + 551.23 + 156.92 + 379.69 + 218.31 + 276.92 + 273.23 + 268.31 + 569.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3121.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4946.32} - {3121.99}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1824.33}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1700}  \quad n_{A_1} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1634}  \quad n_{A_2} = {39}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1711}  \quad n_{A_3} = {39}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1700}^2}{39} + \frac{{1634}^2}{39} + \frac{{1711}^2}{39}} - \frac{{5045}^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2890000}}{39} + \frac{{2669956}}{39} + \frac{{2927521}}{39}} - \frac{{25452025}}{117}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{74102.56} + {68460.41} + {75064.64}} - {217538.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {217627.61} - {217538.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {88.93}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{88.93}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {44.46}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{44.46}{28.91}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.54}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{88.93}{{4946.32} - {340.17} - {1395.23}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{88.93}{3210.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {108}) = {1.54}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1645}  \quad n_{B_1} = {39}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1775}  \quad n_{B_2} = {39}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1625}  \quad n_{B_3} = {39}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.08}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1645}^2}{39} + \frac{{1775}^2}{39} + \frac{{1625}^2}{39}} - \frac{{5045}^2}{117}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2706025}}{39} + \frac{{3150625}}{39} + \frac{{2640625}}{39}} - \frac{{25452025}}{117}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{69385.26} + {80785.26} + {67708.33}} - {217538.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {217878.85} - {217538.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {340.17}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{340.17}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {170.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{170.08}{28.91}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.88}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{340.17}{{4946.32} - {88.93} - {1395.23}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{340.17}{3462.16}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {108}) = {5.88}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.46}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1824.33} - {88.93} - {340.17}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1395.23}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1395.23}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {348.81}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{348.81}{28.91}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {12.07}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1395.23}{{4946.32} - {88.93} - {340.17}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1395.23}{4517.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {108}) = {12.07}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 13
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {399} \quad M_{A1B1} = {33.25}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {326.25} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {433} \quad M_{A1B2} = {36.08}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {260.92} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {426} \quad M_{A1B3} = {35.5}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {383.0} \quad n_{A1B3} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {400} \quad M_{A2B1} = {33.33}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {318.67} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {486} \quad M_{A2B2} = {40.5}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {321.0} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {405} \quad M_{A2B3} = {33.75}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {210.25} \quad n_{A2B3} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {72} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {71}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {72} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {66}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {92519} - \frac{2549^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {92519} - \frac{6497401}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {92519} - {90241.68}\)  \(SS_{total} = {2277.32}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {326.25 + 260.92 + 383.0 + 318.67 + 321.0 + 210.25}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1820.09}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2277.32} - {1820.09}\)  \(SS_{between} = {457.23}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1258}  \quad n_{A_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1291}  \quad n_{A_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.99}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1258}^2}{36} + \frac{{1291}^2}{36}} - \frac{{2549}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1582564}}{36} + \frac{{1666681}}{36}} - \frac{{6497401}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{43960.11} + {46296.69}} - {90241.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {90256.8} - {90241.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {15.12}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{15.12}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {15.12}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{15.12}{27.58}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.55}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{15.12}{{2277.32} - {321.78} - {120.33}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{15.12}{1835.21}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {0.55}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {799}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {919}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {831}  \quad n_{B_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{799}^2}{24} + \frac{{919}^2}{24} + \frac{{831}^2}{24}} - \frac{{2549}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{638401}}{24} + \frac{{844561}}{24} + \frac{{690561}}{24}} - \frac{{6497401}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{26600.04} + {35190.04} + {28773.38}} - {90241.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {90563.46} - {90241.68}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {321.78}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{321.78}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {160.89}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{160.89}{27.58}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {5.83}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{321.78}{{2277.32} - {15.12} - {120.33}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{321.78}{2141.87}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.15}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {5.83}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.15}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {457.23} - {15.12} - {321.78}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {120.33}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{120.33}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {60.16}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{60.16}{27.58}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{120.33}{{2277.32} - {15.12} - {321.78}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{120.33}{1940.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {2.18}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 14
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {165} \quad M_{A1B1} = {20.62}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {135.88} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {128} \quad M_{A1B2} = {16.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {90.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {215} \quad M_{A2B1} = {26.88}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {136.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {169} \quad M_{A2B2} = {21.12}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {120.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {32} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {31}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {32} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {28}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15283} - \frac{677^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15283} - \frac{458329}{32}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15283} - {14322.78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {960.22}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {135.88 + 90.0 + 136.88 + 120.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {483.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {960.22} - {483.64}\)  \(SS_{between} = {476.58}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {293}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {384}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{293}^2}{16} + \frac{{384}^2}{16}} - \frac{{677}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{85849}}{16} + \frac{{147456}}{16}} - \frac{{458329}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{5365.56} + {9216.0}} - {14322.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {14581.56} - {14322.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {258.78}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{258.78}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {258.78}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{258.78}{17.27}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {14.98}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{258.78}{{960.22} - {215.28} - {2.52}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{258.78}{742.42}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {14.98}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.35}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {380}  \quad n_{B_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {297}  \quad n_{B_2} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{380}^2}{16} + \frac{{297}^2}{16}} - \frac{{677}^2}{32}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{144400}}{16} + \frac{{88209}}{16}} - \frac{{458329}}{32}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{9025.0} + {5513.06}} - {14322.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {14538.06} - {14322.78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {215.28}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{215.28}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {215.28}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{215.28}{17.27}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {12.47}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{215.28}{{960.22} - {258.78} - {2.52}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{215.28}{698.92}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {12.47}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.64}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {476.58} - {258.78} - {215.28}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2.52}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2.52}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2.52}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2.52}{17.27}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.15}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.52}{{960.22} - {258.78} - {215.28}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.52}{486.16}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {28}) = {0.15}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 15
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {153} \quad M_{A1B1} = {12.75}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {38.25} \quad n_{A1B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {171} \quad M_{A1B2} = {14.25}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {10.25} \quad n_{A1B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {143} \quad M_{A1B3} = {11.92}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {28.92} \quad n_{A1B3} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {186} \quad M_{A2B1} = {15.5}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {37.0} \quad n_{A2B1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {200} \quad M_{A2B2} = {16.67}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {20.67} \quad n_{A2B2} = {12}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {160} \quad M_{A2B3} = {13.33}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {60.67} \quad n_{A2B3} = {12}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {72} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {71}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {72} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {66}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {14637} - \frac{1013^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {14637} - \frac{1026169}{72}\)  \(SS_{total} = {14637} - {14252.35}\)  \(SS_{total} = {384.65}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {38.25 + 10.25 + 28.92 + 37.0 + 20.67 + 60.67}\)  \(SS_{within} = {195.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {384.65} - {195.76}\)  \(SS_{between} = {188.89}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {467}  \quad n_{A_1} = {36}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {546}  \quad n_{A_2} = {36}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.99}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{467}^2}{36} + \frac{{546}^2}{36}} - \frac{{1013}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{218089}}{36} + \frac{{298116}}{36}} - \frac{{1026169}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{6058.03} + {8281.0}} - {14252.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {14339.03} - {14252.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {86.68}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{86.68}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {86.68}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{86.68}{2.97}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {29.19}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{86.68}{{384.65} - {96.45} - {5.76}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{86.68}{282.44}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {29.19}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {339}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {371}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {303}  \quad n_{B_3} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{339}^2}{24} + \frac{{371}^2}{24} + \frac{{303}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1013}^2}{72}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{114921}}{24} + \frac{{137641}}{24} + \frac{{91809}}{24}} - \frac{{1026169}}{72}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{4788.38} + {5735.04} + {3825.38}} - {14252.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {14348.8} - {14252.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {96.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{96.45}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {48.22}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{48.22}{2.97}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {16.24}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{96.45}{{384.65} - {86.68} - {5.76}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{96.45}{292.21}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.33}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {16.24}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.33}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {188.89} - {86.68} - {96.45}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {5.76}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{5.76}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {2.88}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{2.88}{2.97}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.97}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.76}{{384.65} - {86.68} - {96.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{5.76}{201.52}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {0.97}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {101866} - \frac{2706^2}{78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {101866} - \frac{7322436}{78}\)  \(SS_{total} = {101866} - {93877.38}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7988.62}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {773.23 + 855.08 + 750.31 + 1848.31 + 648.77 + 1289.08}\)  \(SS_{within} = {6164.78}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7988.62} - {6164.78}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1823.84}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{702.0}{85.62}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {8.2}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{702.0}{{7988.62} - {385.16} - {736.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{702.0}{6866.78}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {72}) = {8.2}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {835}  \quad n_{B_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {976}  \quad n_{B_2} = {26}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {895}  \quad n_{B_3} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{835}^2}{26} + \frac{{976}^2}{26} + \frac{{895}^2}{26}} - \frac{{2706}^2}{78}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{697225}}{26} + \frac{{952576}}{26} + \frac{{801025}}{26}} - \frac{{7322436}}{78}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{26816.35} + {36637.54} + {30808.65}} - {93877.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {94262.54} - {93877.38}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {385.16}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{385.16}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {192.58}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{192.58}{85.62}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.25}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{385.16}{{7988.62} - {702.0} - {736.68}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{385.16}{6549.94}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {2.25}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1823.84} - {702.0} - {385.16}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {736.68}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{736.68}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {368.34}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{368.34}{85.62}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.3}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{736.68}{{7988.62} - {702.0} - {385.16}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{736.68}{6901.46}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {72}) = {4.3}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56905} - \frac{1053^2}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56905} - \frac{1108809}{20}\)  \(SS_{total} = {56905} - {55440.45}\)  \(SS_{total} = {1464.55}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {291.2 + 202.8 + 26.8 + 142.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {663.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1464.55} - {663.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = {800.95}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.53}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {800.95} - {48.05} - {684.45}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {68.45}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{68.45}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {68.45}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{68.45}{41.48}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.65}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{68.45}{{1464.55} - {48.05} - {684.45}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{68.45}{732.05}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {16}) = {1.65}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {88884} - \frac{2130^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {88884} - \frac{4536900}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {88884} - {84016.67}\)  \(SS_{total} = {4867.33}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {323.56 + 837.56 + 1024.89 + 870.0 + 398.22 + 876.89}\)  \(SS_{within} = {4331.12}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {4867.33} - {4331.12}\)  \(SS_{between} = {536.21}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{57.72}{90.23}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.64}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{115.44}{{4867.33} - {303.41} - {117.36}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{115.44}{4446.56}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {0.64}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\)

text:

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{190.74}{12.09}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {15.78}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {87068} - \frac{2156^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {87068} - \frac{4648336}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {87068} - {86080.3}\)  \(SS_{total} = {987.7}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {62.0 + 115.56 + 56.0 + 44.22 + 54.0 + 68.89}\)  \(SS_{within} = {400.67}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {987.7} - {400.67}\)  \(SS_{between} = {587.03}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{201.86}{8.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {24.17}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{21.4}{8.35}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.56}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{21.4}{{987.7} - {403.71} - {161.92}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{21.4}{422.07}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {2.56}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {587.03} - {403.71} - {21.4}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {161.92}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{161.92}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {80.96}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{80.96}{8.35}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {9.7}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{161.92}{{987.7} - {403.71} - {21.4}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{161.92}{562.59}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {9.7}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\)

Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {160}  \quad n_{A_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {166}  \quad n_{A_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{160}^2}{12} + \frac{{166}^2}{12}} - \frac{{326}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{25600}}{12} + \frac{{27556}}{12}} - \frac{{106276}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{2133.33} + {2296.33}} - {4428.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {4429.66} - {4428.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1.49}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.49}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {1.49}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{1.49}{1.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.81}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.49}{{87.83} - {16.66} - {32.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1.49}{38.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.81}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {173}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {153}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{173}^2}{12} + \frac{{153}^2}{12}} - \frac{{326}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{29929}}{12} + \frac{{23409}}{12}} - \frac{{106276}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{2494.08} + {1950.75}} - {4428.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {4444.83} - {4428.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {16.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{16.66}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {16.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{16.66}{1.85}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {9.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{{87.83} - {1.49} - {32.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{53.65}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.31}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {9.01}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.31}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {8.1}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {50.84} - {1.49} - {16.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {32.69}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{32.69}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {32.69}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{32.69}{1.85}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {17.67}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{32.69}{{87.83} - {1.49} - {16.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{32.69}{69.68}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.47}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {17.67}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.47}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 27
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {120} \quad M_{A1B1} = {10.91}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {52.91} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {128} \quad M_{A1B2} = {11.64}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {36.55} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {131} \quad M_{A2B1} = {11.91}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {40.91} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {191} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.36}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {36.55} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {147} \quad M_{A3B1} = {13.36}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {40.55} \quad n_{A3B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {142} \quad M_{A3B2} = {12.91}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {52.91} \quad n_{A3B2} = {11}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {66} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {65}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {66} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {60}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {11733} - \frac{859^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {11733} - \frac{737881}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {11733} - {11180.02}\)  \(SS_{total} = {552.98}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {52.91 + 36.55 + 40.91 + 36.55 + 40.55 + 52.91}\)  \(SS_{within} = {260.38}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {552.98} - {260.38}\)  \(SS_{between} = {292.6}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {248}  \quad n_{A_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {322}  \quad n_{A_2} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {289}  \quad n_{A_3} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{248}^2}{22} + \frac{{322}^2}{22} + \frac{{289}^2}{22}} - \frac{{859}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{61504}}{22} + \frac{{103684}}{22} + \frac{{83521}}{22}} - \frac{{737881}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{2795.64} + {4712.91} + {3796.41}} - {11180.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {11304.96} - {11180.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {124.94}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{124.94}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {62.47}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{62.47}{4.34}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {14.39}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{124.94}{{552.98} - {60.13} - {107.53}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{124.94}{385.32}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.32}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {14.39}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.32}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {398}  \quad n_{B_1} = {33}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {461}  \quad n_{B_2} = {33}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.0}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{398}^2}{33} + \frac{{461}^2}{33}} - \frac{{859}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{158404}}{33} + \frac{{212521}}{33}} - \frac{{737881}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{4800.12} + {6440.03}} - {11180.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {11240.15} - {11180.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {60.13}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{60.13}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {60.13}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{60.13}{4.34}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {13.85}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.13}{{552.98} - {124.94} - {107.53}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.13}{320.51}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.19}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {60}) = {13.85}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.19}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {292.6} - {124.94} - {60.13}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {107.53}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{107.53}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {53.76}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{53.76}{4.34}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {12.39}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{107.53}{{552.98} - {124.94} - {60.13}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{107.53}{367.91}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.29}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {12.39}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.29}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{462.25}{{1992.75} - {34.03} - {140.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{462.25}{1818.69}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.25}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {10.9}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.25}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.15}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {636.31} - {34.03} - {462.25}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {140.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{140.03}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {140.03}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{140.03}{42.39}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {3.3}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{140.03}{{1992.75} - {34.03} - {462.25}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{140.03}{1496.47}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {32}) = {3.3}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 29
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {743} \quad M_{A1B1} = {49.53}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {447.73} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {839} \quad M_{A1B2} = {55.93}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {396.93} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {963} \quad M_{A2B1} = {64.2}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {874.4} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {901} \quad M_{A2B2} = {60.07}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {486.93} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {718} \quad M_{A3B1} = {47.87}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {345.73} \quad n_{A3B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {885} \quad M_{A3B2} = {59.0}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {320.0} \quad n_{A3B2} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {90} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {89}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {90} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {84}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {289131} - \frac{5049^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {289131} - \frac{25492401}{90}\)  \(SS_{total} = {289131} - {283248.9}\)  \(SS_{total} = {5882.1}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {447.73 + 396.93 + 874.4 + 486.93 + 345.73 + 320.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2871.72}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {5882.1} - {2871.72}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3010.38}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1582}  \quad n_{A_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1864}  \quad n_{A_2} = {30}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1603}  \quad n_{A_3} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1582}^2}{30} + \frac{{1864}^2}{30} + \frac{{1603}^2}{30}} - \frac{{5049}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2502724}}{30} + \frac{{3474496}}{30} + \frac{{2569609}}{30}} - \frac{{25492401}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{83424.13} + {115816.53} + {85653.63}} - {283248.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {284894.29} - {283248.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {1645.39}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{1645.39}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {822.7}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{822.7}{34.19}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {24.06}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1645.39}{{5882.1} - {448.9} - {916.09}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1645.39}{4517.11}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {24.06}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.36}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {2424}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {2625}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.95}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2424}^2}{45} + \frac{{2625}^2}{45}} - \frac{{5049}^2}{90}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{5875776}}{45} + \frac{{6890625}}{45}} - \frac{{25492401}}{90}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{130572.8} + {153125.0}} - {283248.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {283697.8} - {283248.9}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {448.9}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{448.9}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {448.9}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{448.9}{34.19}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {13.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{448.9}{{5882.1} - {1645.39} - {916.09}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{448.9}{3320.62}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {84}) = {13.13}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.14}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3010.38} - {1645.39} - {448.9}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {916.09}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{916.09}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {458.04}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{458.04}{34.19}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {13.4}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{916.09}{{5882.1} - {1645.39} - {448.9}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{916.09}{3787.81}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {84}) = {13.4}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 30
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {510} \quad M_{A1B1} = {34.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {872.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {364} \quad M_{A1B2} = {24.27}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {832.93} \quad n_{A1B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {481} \quad M_{A1B3} = {32.07}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {990.93} \quad n_{A1B3} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {491} \quad M_{A2B1} = {32.73}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {882.93} \quad n_{A2B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {367} \quad M_{A2B2} = {24.47}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {829.73} \quad n_{A2B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {415} \quad M_{A2B3} = {27.67}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {611.33} \quad n_{A2B3} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {432} \quad M_{A3B1} = {28.8}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {508.4} \quad n_{A3B1} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {563} \quad M_{A3B2} = {37.53}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {519.73} \quad n_{A3B2} = {15}\)  \(T_{A3B3} = {378} \quad M_{A3B3} = {25.2}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {970.4} \quad n_{A3B3} = {15}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {135} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {134}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {9} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {8}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {135} - {9}\)  \(df_{within} = {126}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128247} - \frac{4001^2}{135}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128247} - \frac{16008001}{135}\)  \(SS_{total} = {128247} - {118577.79}\)  \(SS_{total} = {9669.21}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {872.0 + 832.93 + 990.93 + 882.93 + 829.73 + 611.33 + 508.4 + 519.73 + 970.4}\)  \(SS_{within} = {7018.38}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {9669.21} - {7018.38}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2650.83}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1355}  \quad n_{A_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1273}  \quad n_{A_2} = {45}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {1373}  \quad n_{A_3} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1355}^2}{45} + \frac{{1273}^2}{45} + \frac{{1373}^2}{45}} - \frac{{4001}^2}{135}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1836025}}{45} + \frac{{1620529}}{45} + \frac{{1885129}}{45}} - \frac{{16008001}}{135}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{40800.56} + {36011.76} + {41891.76}} - {118577.79}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {118704.08} - {118577.79}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {126.29}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{126.29}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {63.14}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{63.14}{55.7}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.13}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{126.29}{{9669.21} - {333.35} - {2191.19}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{126.29}{7144.67}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {126}) = {1.13}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1433}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1294}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {1274}  \quad n_{B_3} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1433}^2}{45} + \frac{{1294}^2}{45} + \frac{{1274}^2}{45}} - \frac{{4001}^2}{135}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2053489}}{45} + \frac{{1674436}}{45} + \frac{{1623076}}{45}} - \frac{{16008001}}{135}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{45633.09} + {37209.69} + {36068.36}} - {118577.79}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {118911.14} - {118577.79}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {333.35}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{333.35}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {166.68}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{166.68}{55.7}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.99}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{333.35}{{9669.21} - {126.29} - {2191.19}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{333.35}{7351.73}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {126}) = {2.99}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.44}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {2650.83} - {126.29} - {333.35}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {2191.19}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{2191.19}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {547.8}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{547.8}{55.7}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {9.83}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2191.19}{{9669.21} - {126.29} - {333.35}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2191.19}{9209.57}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {126}) = {9.83}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\)
